name: VDT Studio 20-KPI Provider Evaluation Dataset
description: Credential-free KPI briefs and deterministic expectations for provider evaluation. The KPI set mirrors Workstream J in the seamless local AI and alpha plan.

cases.goal: Increase saleable monthly output without adding new equipment.
cases.businessContext: Open-pit operation with planned maintenance, unplanned downtime and variable plant productivity.

prohibitedDuplicatePatterns: production volume repeated as an input driver
downtime split into identical duplicate nodes
formulaExpectations: Production Volume should connect productive time and productivity/rate drivers.
unitConsistencyExpectations: Root unit should remain a volume per period.

cases.goal: Lift OEE for a bottleneck production line.
cases.businessContext: Line losses come from availability, speed losses, quality defects and changeover time.

prohibitedDuplicatePatterns: availability and uptime as separate identical drivers
quality rate and good count ratio duplicated
formulaExpectations: OEE should be represented as availability multiplied by performance and quality.
unitConsistencyExpectations: Root and component rates should use percentage or ratio units consistently.

cases.goal: Increase generating asset availability during peak demand months.
cases.businessContext: Availability is driven by planned outages, forced outages, maintenance duration and startup reliability.

prohibitedDuplicatePatterns: downtime repeated under multiple names without distinct causes
formulaExpectations: Availability should compare available operating time with scheduled or calendar time.
unitConsistencyExpectations: Time components should use compatible hour-based units.

cases.goal: Reduce maintenance spend without increasing equipment risk.
cases.businessContext: Cost is influenced by labor, parts, contractor work, planned work mix and emergency repairs.

prohibitedDuplicatePatterns: planned maintenance and scheduled maintenance as identical children
formulaExpectations: Maintenance cost should add cost categories and may separate planned versus reactive work.
unitConsistencyExpectations: Cost components should use the same currency per period.

cases.goal: Lower cost per tonne while preserving throughput and recovery.
cases.businessContext: Unit cost depends on total operating cost, production volume, energy, reagents, labor and maintenance.

prohibitedDuplicatePatterns: total cost repeated as a child of itself
volume and throughput duplicated without distinction
formulaExpectations: Unit Cost should divide total cost by production or throughput volume.
unitConsistencyExpectations: Numerator should be currency per period and denominator should be volume per period.

cases.goal: Reduce excess inventory while maintaining fulfillment reliability.
cases.businessContext: Inventory changes with beginning stock, receipts, demand, returns, scrap and safety stock policy.

prohibitedDuplicatePatterns: demand and sales used as duplicate outflow nodes
formulaExpectations: Inventory should reconcile opening stock plus inflows minus outflows.
unitConsistencyExpectations: Inventory, receipts and demand should use compatible unit counts.

cases.goal: Improve customer service level across regional lanes.
cases.businessContext: Service is affected by order fill, on-time delivery, stockouts, carrier capacity and exception handling.

prohibitedDuplicatePatterns: on-time delivery repeated as service level itself
formulaExpectations: Service Level should be framed as successful orders or deliveries over total demand.
unitConsistencyExpectations: Rate components should use percentage or count-over-count logic.

cases.goal: Release cash tied up in operations without disrupting supply.
cases.businessContext: Working capital is shaped by receivables, inventory, payables, payment terms and forecast accuracy.

prohibitedDuplicatePatterns: cash and working capital shown as the same root calculation
formulaExpectations: Working Capital should combine receivables and inventory less payables.
unitConsistencyExpectations: Balance-sheet components should use the same currency.

cases.goal: Improve EBITDA through revenue growth and operating cost control.
cases.businessContext: EBITDA depends on revenue, cost of goods sold, operating expenses, mix and one-off adjustments.

prohibitedDuplicatePatterns: gross profit and EBITDA used as interchangeable duplicate nodes
formulaExpectations: EBITDA should subtract operating costs from revenue before financing, tax, depreciation and amortization effects.
unitConsistencyExpectations: Revenue and cost components should use the same currency per period.

cases.goal: Grow monthly revenue without relying solely on discounts.
cases.businessContext: Revenue is affected by traffic, conversion, average order value, price, product mix and returns.

prohibitedDuplicatePatterns: sales and revenue as identical siblings
formulaExpectations: Revenue should connect sales volume and price, with returns or discounts where relevant.
unitConsistencyExpectations: Price and volume should resolve to currency per period.

cases.goal: Improve customer retention across enterprise accounts.
cases.businessContext: Retention depends on renewals, churn, product adoption, support quality and account expansion risk.

prohibitedDuplicatePatterns: retention and renewal rate duplicated without a clear distinction
formulaExpectations: Retention should compare retained customers or revenue with the starting base.
unitConsistencyExpectations: Customer-count or revenue-base denominators should be explicit.

cases.goal: Increase purchase conversion from site visits.
cases.businessContext: Conversion is influenced by qualified traffic, product availability, checkout friction, pricing and page speed.

prohibitedDuplicatePatterns: orders and conversions repeated as separate identical drivers
formulaExpectations: Conversion Rate should divide successful conversions by eligible visitors or sessions.
unitConsistencyExpectations: Numerator and denominator should be compatible counts.

cases.goal: Shorten average customer delivery time without increasing expedite cost.
cases.businessContext: Delivery time includes order processing, warehouse pick/pack, dispatch wait, transit and last-mile handoff.

prohibitedDuplicatePatterns: lead time and delivery time used as identical sibling drivers
formulaExpectations: Delivery Time should add sequential time components or weighted lane times.
unitConsistencyExpectations: All time components should use compatible time units.

cases.goal: Reduce recordable incident rate across operating crews.
cases.businessContext: Incident rate is affected by exposure hours, unsafe conditions, training compliance, permit quality and near-miss reporting.

prohibitedDuplicatePatterns: incident count duplicated as incident rate
formulaExpectations: Safety Incident Rate should normalize incidents by exposure hours.
unitConsistencyExpectations: Incident count and exposure-hour denominator should be explicit.

cases.goal: Reduce energy consumption while preserving production output.
cases.businessContext: Energy use is driven by equipment load, operating hours, efficiency, standby losses and process conditions.

prohibitedDuplicatePatterns: power and energy used interchangeably without time conversion
formulaExpectations: Energy Consumption should connect power/load with operating time and efficiency losses.
unitConsistencyExpectations: Power multiplied by time should resolve to energy units.

cases.goal: Improve metal recovery while keeping throughput stable.
cases.businessContext: Recovery depends on feed grade, grind size, reagent dosing, residence time and circuit stability.

prohibitedDuplicatePatterns: recovery and yield duplicated without process distinction
formulaExpectations: Recovery should compare recovered valuable material with material available in feed.
unitConsistencyExpectations: Recovered and feed metal should use compatible mass units.

cases.goal: Increase plant throughput while staying within quality constraints.
cases.businessContext: Throughput is influenced by feed availability, equipment capacity, bottleneck utilization, downtime and process constraints.

prohibitedDuplicatePatterns: capacity and throughput duplicated without utilization logic
formulaExpectations: Throughput should connect available operating time, capacity/rate and utilization losses.
unitConsistencyExpectations: Rate, time and output units should be internally consistent.

cases.goal: Increase first-pass yield on a high-volume line.
cases.businessContext: Yield is affected by input material quality, process settings, defects, rework and scrap.

prohibitedDuplicatePatterns: yield and quality rate duplicated without a defined difference
formulaExpectations: Yield should compare good output with total input or started units.
unitConsistencyExpectations: Good output, scrap and input should use compatible units.

cases.goal: Increase realized procurement savings without increasing supply risk.
cases.businessContext: Savings depend on negotiated price changes, volume compliance, demand management, supplier rebates and leakage.

prohibitedDuplicatePatterns: savings and cost avoidance merged without labeling
formulaExpectations: Procurement Savings should compare baseline and actual spend with volume/compliance treatment.
unitConsistencyExpectations: Baseline, actual and savings values should use the same currency per period.

cases.goal: Increase output per FTE while preserving quality and safety.
cases.businessContext: Productivity depends on output volume, staffing, effective working time, skill mix, absenteeism and rework.

prohibitedDuplicatePatterns: headcount and FTE repeated as identical nodes
formulaExpectations: Workforce Productivity should divide output by FTE, hours or labor input.
unitConsistencyExpectations: Output and labor denominator should produce a productivity ratio.
